name: Chocolate Silk Mousse Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F.
2. Butter the bottom and sides of an 8-inch springform pan. Line the bottom with parchment or waxed paper. Wrap foil under and up outside of the spring form pan; set aside.
3. In a saucepan, combine chocolate and butter. Cook over low heat, stirring often, until melted and smooth. Remove from heat and cool for 15 minutes.
4. Beat eggs and sugar in hot water (not boiling) with whisk until eggs are lukewarm and foamy and sugar is dissolved. Transfer to a large bowl.
5. Beat egg mixture at high speed until very thick and pale yellow, about 8 minutes. Add flour and fold into the cooled chocolate mixture.
6. Pour batter into prepared pan. Fill pan with hot water in a roasting pan so it comes halfway up the side of the springform pan. Bake for 15 minutes (center will remain soft).
7. Remove cake from foil but keep the side of the springform pan attached. Cool at room temperature for 1 hour.
8. Refrigerate overnight or up to 3 days.
9. On serving day, prepare glaze: In a small saucepan, combine heavy cream and chocolate. Cook over low heat, stirring often, until smooth. Chill until spreadable but still pourable, about 20 minutes.
10. Loosen the cake from the springform pan, invert onto a plate, and remove bottom pan and parchment paper. Spread glaze on the side of the cake, then pour remaining glaze over top to form a smooth surface. Let set for 30 minutes before decorating with white candy roses.
